title: Minimal-Waste 1x8 Shop Hutch
summary: A wall-hung shop hutch built entirely from 1x8s with almost no waste — angled bottom edges on the sides, adjustable-height middle shelves, and a cleat-hung mounting system.
description:
This hutch is designed around getting the most out of a short stack of 1x8s, with a cut list sized to leave almost no offcuts — a genuinely practical build for a shop or garage wall rather than a showpiece. The two sides get a simple visual softening at the bottom: trim back 2" and 2" with a circular saw, jigsaw, or a miter saw set to 45°, rather than leaving a hard square corner at floor-facing height.

The shelves attach directly to the sides and the bottom 1x4, and the middle shelves are explicitly adjustable — position them at whatever height suits the tools, jars, or supplies you're storing, rather than following a single fixed spacing. That flexibility is the point of a shop hutch: shop storage needs change more often than living-room storage does.

The back 1x4 is inset with glue and becomes the entire hanging system — **this hutch has no separate cleat or bracket, so that back board must land on and be screwed directly into real wall studs.** A hutch loaded with tools or jars is genuine dead weight on that one connection.

materials.name: Wood screws, 3", for hanging into studs

cutList.note: Bottom front corner trimmed back 2" and up 2".
Inset with glue; this board is the entire hanging system.

steps.title: Shape and prep the sides
steps.body: Trim back the bottom front corner of each side board 2" and up 2" with a circular saw, jigsaw, or a miter saw set to 45°. Attach the 1x4 bottom to the sides.

steps.title: Add the shelves
steps.body: Attach all shelves to the sides and the bottom 1x4. Position the middle shelves at whatever height suits your storage needs.

steps.title: Add the back support
steps.body: Attach the back 1x4, inset with glue. This board is the entire hanging system, so make sure it's securely attached to the sides and shelves.

steps.title: Hang the hutch
steps.body: Locate wall studs and attach the hutch directly to the studs with 3" wood screws through the back support.
materials: Wood screws, 3", for hanging into studs

unresolvedImages.alt: Wall-mounted shop hutch built from 1x8 boards
